area: Task scope amendment / Windows CLI parsing
finding: On Windows/PowerShell, `tasks scope add --add "path1,path2"` normalized the live allowedFiles list but persisted quote-contaminated paths in amendmentHistory, leaving audit history with entries like `"packages/...` and `...test.ts"`.
expectedBehavior: Scope amendment parsing should canonicalize paths once and persist the same clean path list to allowedFiles and amendmentHistory. The CLI should reject or repair malformed quoted path fragments and expose a supported status/preflight command for scope audit.
evidenceOrRepro: Plan 3.1 ATM-GOV-0258 scope amendment report: allowedFiles was clean, but amendmentHistory transition 2026-07-22T15-11-19-606Z contained quote-contaminated first/last addedPaths; `tasks scope status/preflight` returned ATM_CLI_USAGE and only listed add/remove/repair/repair-deliverables.
followUp: Implement malformed scope-amendment path regression coverage in ATM-GOV-0259; add parser-level quote-fragment rejection/repair and a discoverable scope audit/status command.
